patterns.title: Verify URLs before sharing (deploy -> verify -> Gatekeeper -> share)
patterns.when: Any time a link/tunnel is shared to Xiaosong or posted as 'ready'.
do: Confirm the URL returns HTTP 200 for the exact asset/page (not just HTML shell).
If using tunnels (trycloudflare/serveo), re-check after 1-2 minutes to catch flakiness.
Gatekeeper must verify before sending to Xiaosong.
avoid: Multiple agents posting unverified links in parallel.

patterns.title: Monitoring alerts: single top-level incident, thread updates, and de-dupe
patterns.when: Any automated monitoring/health check posts status updates (ports/gateways).
do: Post exactly 1 top-level incident starter per incident.
Send all follow-ups as thread replies (replyTo=starter) OR edit the starter message.
De-dupe repeated statuses within a cool-down window (e.g., 5–10 min) to avoid spam.
If a duplicate top-level message slips out: delete it and summarize in the thread.
avoid: Posting new updates after the parent/starter message is deleted (orphan threads).

patterns.title: Do not use message(action=send) for Slack; use slack-send.ps1 / slack-notify.ps1
patterns.when: Any Slack outbound message (especially from bots/agents).
do: Use shared scripts to ensure correct channel routing and webhook triggers.
Only use message tool for Slack when explicitly safe/no-mentions and routing is verified.
avoid: Accidentally routing replies to Xiaosong DM due to context bleed.

patterns.title: Tunnels are fragile; keep fallback and avoid rapid restarts
do: Have a fallback tunnel ready (cloudflared -> serveo or other).
Avoid rapid dev-server restarts to prevent TIME_WAIT/port exhaustion.
Prefer stable hosting when sharing externally.
